overview: This four-color midrange deck leverages a wide mana base to access efficient disruption and resilient threats. By combining early interaction like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push with the card advantage engine of Quantum Riddler, it aims to stabilize quickly and outvalue opponents. The deck excels at adapting to any matchup by answering key pieces while maintaining a steady board presence.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents with Quantum Riddler's continuous card advantage
Securing early board control through targeted removal and hand disruption
Closing games with efficient four-color threats that pressure the opponent

keep_criteria: Hands containing early disruption like Thoughtseize or Fatal Push
Hands with Quantum Riddler and a cheap play to protect it
Hands with at least two lands and a threat for turns one through three
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including duals/fixing), a Thoughtseize or Fatal Push, and a turn-2/3 threat.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early interaction against aggressive decks
Hands with only high-cost threats and no removal
Hands missing land drops for the first three turns

Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, multiple unplayable removal without threats, or a hand that can't interact with the top meta (e.g., no discard against control/combo, no early removal against Izzet Prowess or Selesnya Company).

early_game: Play lands and use Fatal Push to remove aggressive threats
Cast Thoughtseize to disrupt opponent's combo or key pieces
Develop board with cheap creatures while holding up mana for interaction
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize if you can identify a key threat or combo piece.
Turn 2: Play a fixing land or dual, cast Fatal Push on early aggro threats, or develop a midrange threat like Quantum Riddler.
Prioritize fixing early to enable 4c spells without stalling.
mid_game: Deploy Quantum Riddler to generate card advantage and stabilize
Trade efficiently to maintain board presence
Use four-color flexibility to answer whatever the opponent plays
Balance mana for interaction vs. development.
Don't overcommit to one color early.
Use discard to disrupt opponent's hand while preserving your own threats.
Manage graveyard by avoiding unnecessary self-discard unless it fuels a specific win condition.
Commit threats when you have removal ready for their response.
Hold back if you suspect a sweep or if you need to preserve interaction for a specific threat.
late_game: Let Quantum Riddler draw multiple cards per turn to outvalue opponents
Cast larger threats to close the game
Maintain hand size and resources to outlast control decks
Quantum Riddler and other midrange threats combined with card advantage.
Win by out-resourcing them through discard, removal, and threat generation.
Go all-in when you have board control, sufficient card advantage, and the opponent has no sweepers or interaction left.
Also commit when you can close the game before they can stabilize.

key_cards.name: Quantum Riddler
key_cards.notes: Prioritize keeping it safe; use its draw ability to refill on interaction and threats while maintaining board presence.

key_cards.name: Thoughtseize
key_cards.notes: Target opponent's key combo pieces, win conditions, or early threats to slow their game plan.

key_cards.name: Fatal Push
key_cards.notes: Use to clear low-toughness creatures and aggressive beaters, preserving mana for larger threats or interaction.

sequencing_tips: Hold up Fatal Push before committing mana to threats to avoid being caught off guard
Cast Thoughtseize before playing Quantum Riddler to protect it from early removal
Prioritize keeping lands in hand when facing control decks to ensure Riddler can draw safely
Quantum Riddler's draw/discard ability synergizes with discard outlets and card advantage engines.
Avoid over-drawing if you have no way to discard.
Be mindful of mana base requirements
ensure you have enough fixing to cast 4c spells consistently.
Common mistake: overcommitting to interaction and running out of threats.

matchups.advice: Use Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to stall until Quantum Riddler stabilizes the board.
Play around board wipes by keeping lands in hand and deploying Riddler on their turn.
Disrupt their key pieces immediately with Thoughtseize and pressure them before they assemble their win condition.

Use discard to remove counterspells. Cast threats in response to their counterspells if possible. Use cheap, efficient threats that are hard to counter (e.g., instant-speed interaction, or threats that don't rely on a single spell).
Early interaction is key. Use Thoughtseize to remove ramp spells. Use Fatal Push to kill early threats. Don't let them get ahead in mana; pressure them with threats.
Win through better card advantage and efficient trades. Use discard to disrupt their hand. Prioritize keeping threats on board. Use removal efficiently.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Cyclonic Rift), combo pieces, and recursion engines.
Note when opponents tap out for big threats or hold up mana for counterspells.
Track graveyard usage for midrange/recursion decks.
